summary: MetaMiner is a metabologenomic pipeline which integrates metabolomic and genomic data to identify RiPPs and biosynthetic gene clusters encoding them.
MetaMiner accepts raw nucleotide sequences in .fasta format as genomic input. MetaMiner accepts predicted and translated RiPP amino acid sequences in .fasta format as genomic input.
MetaMiner identifies putative BGCs and corresponding precursor peptides as a first pipeline step.
MetaMiner constructs putative RiPP structure databases as a second pipeline step.
MetaMiner matches tandem mass spectra against the constructed post-translationally modified RiPPs structure database using Dereplicator as a third pipeline step.
MetaMiner enlarges the set of described RiPPs via spectral networking as a fourth pipeline step.
MetaMiner default search mode considers RiPP class as 'lantibiotic'.
MetaMiner identified a lantibiotic with original sequence TGSQVSLLVCEYSSLSVVLCTP in test data.
The test sequence after modifications shows as T-18GS-18QVS-18LLVCEYS-18SLSVVLCTP in MetaMiner output.
The modifications T-18 and S-18 correspond to dehydrobutyrine and dehydroalanine respectively.
The test sequences correspond to AmfS peptide.
MetaMiner accepts nucleotide or amino acid FASTA files with extensions .fna, .fasta, or .fa.
